details.response_excerpt:
The natural linewidth ofa state with lifetime τ is roughly ΔE ≈ ħ/τ.  
For τ = 10⁻⁹ s, ΔE₁ ≈ 6.6 × 10⁻⁷ eV.  
For τ = 10⁻⁸ s, ΔE₂ ≈ 6.6 × 10⁻⁸ eV.  

To resolve two levels, their energy separation must exceed the larger linewidth, i.e., be > ~10⁻⁶ eV. Among the given choices, only 10⁻⁴ eV satisfies this requirement.

ANSWER: A

[REASONING]

details.response_excerpt:
ANSWER:B

[REASONING]
We need to compute expectation value of operator O = 10 σ_z + 5 σ_x for a spin-1/2 particle in state |ψ> = 0.5 |↑> + sqrt(3)/2 |↓>. Note that |↑> and |↓> are eigenstates of σ_z with eigenvalues +1 and -1 respectively. Also σ_x has matrix representation that flips spin: σ_x|↑> = |↓>, σ_x|↓> = |↑>. So expectation value <ψ| (10 σ_z + 5 σ_x) |ψ> = 10 <ψ|σ_z|ψ> + 5 <ψ|σ_x|ψ>.
